entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

My name is Quy and I am a VietNamese women. Now I am working as SAP CRM consultant.

I identify myself as: People looking to switch their careers mid-life.

I already finished the learning journey "Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Sales – Fundamental Business Processes". In the future, I want to boost my career with SD skills.

Have a chance to take and complete sap certification is my idea to switch to SD module.

Hope receive feedback from you soon!

entry.title: Transforming Business Operations with an AI Copilot
entry.content:
AI is rapidly changing the landscape of business operations across various industries. One of the key applications of AI in businesses today are AI copilots. These AI-powered tools are designed to understand, learn, and respond to human language in a contextual manner. They offer numerous benefits, including operational efficiency, automating manual tasks, improved customer engagement, and data-driven decision-making.

AI copilots work by utilizing advanced algorithms and machine learning techniques to understand and respond to user queries and requests. They are designed to process and analyze vast amounts of data in real-time, enabling them to generate accurate and relevant responses. They rely on natural language processing to comprehend the nuances of human language and extract meaning from text or speech input. AI copilots continuously learn and improve their performance. They are trained on massive datasets, which let them recognize patterns, make predictions, and provide personalized recommendations.

SAP Business AI is already embedded in the SAP applications that customers already use to run their most critical business processes. With SAP Business AI, you are empowered to interact with SAP software through Joule – SAP’s copilot available across SAP applications.

Joule lets you become more efficient and productive through simply describing your ideas, asking analytical questions, or telling the system what to do. With Joule, you can:

  • Streamline tasks across SAP applications
  • Get quick answers and smart insights on-demand
  • Get tailored content to jumpstart your work
  • Maintain full control over decision-making and your data privacy

The adoption of AI copilots in business operations is no longer a novelty but a necessity for staying competitive in the digital age. From improving customer service to enhancing operational efficiency and enabling data-driven decisions, AI assistants offer a myriad of benefits. As AI technology continues to evolve, businesses can expect even more sophisticated capabilities from AI copilots, reshaping the way businesses operate and interact with their customers and employees.

Interesting in learning more?

  • Learn more about SAP Business AI
  • Meet Joule, SAP’s AI copilot
  • What is generative AI?

entry.title: Live Data Connectivity to Google Big Query
entry.content:
Good news for customers looking for live data access to Google BigQuery (GBQ)! With the release in Q4 of 2024, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) plans to get its first live SQL data access. Google BigQuery (GBQ) will be the first SQL data source consumed by SAC, as part of SAP’s partnership with Google.

Traditionally, live data access in SAC requires that the remote data source is a data warehouse, completed by semantic modelling and an OLAP engine to access the complete data model. With live SQL data access, SAC will be able to directly access fact data from customers' GBQ data lake, giving customers using GBQ the flexibility to decide whether they want to use acquired or live data within their models. With that, customers will no longer need to maintain load jobs from their data lakes.

Users will be able to choose between using a GBQ service user to access their data lake or individual authentication if user-based security is being used on the data lake. To maximize performance, SAC will use SQL pushdown wherever the source database can handle it.”

entry.title: Re: Customer / Supplier Business Partner Master Data: Authorization Groups
entry.content:
Hi @Stephen_Ward, 

I am in a case where we have two companies in the system, and the users that manage one company should not be able to see or modify the business partners of the other company, not even the basic address data. For this, it has been proposed to create two groupings of business partners, and to be able to restrict the visibility according to the grouping; and the problem is that we have not seen that this restriction can be used. At the basic data level of the business partner, there are only the authorization groups, but it does not work, since it is not possible to restrict according to the company.

Can someone help me to solve it? 

#bp #businesspartnerrestriction #saps4hanapubliccloud

Thanks a lot,

Regards

entry.title: Generate Custom Legal Certificates in SAP DRC
entry.content:
The purpose of withholding tax is to ensure that the government receives tax revenue in a timely manner and to facilitate tax compliance. It is commonly applied to various types of income such as wages, salaries, interest, dividends, royalties, and payments to contractors or freelancers. The rates of withholding tax and the types of income subject to withholding tax vary depending on the tax laws of each jurisdiction.

While SAP provides standard reports for reporting withholding tax, custom requirements may arise for specific needs. This blog focuses on the development of a custom certificate to address such requirements. Developing a custom certificate requires knowledge in DRC extensibility and Adobe Form development.

In this scenario, a custom Adobe form is being created within the system, extending the Thailand 50 BIS certificate.

Go to t-code SFP to create the Adobe Form. The form can be developed using the default interface SOMU_DEFAULT_INTERFACE, with XFA2 chosen as the layout technology.

Once the form layout is created, save and activate it.
Then, navigate to Define Compliance Application and initiate the creation of a new report definition. For this example, an existing report definition is duplicated. Provide a name for the new report definition and specify a package for its storage.

Create custom report definition

 

Save it in local package

After creating a new report definition, Add a new document according to the specified requirements. In this instance, a PDF document is presented as an example.

Add new legal document

 

Create it as a PDF

Provide details for the document such as the document name, file name, code page, etc.

Document Details

Select a schema for this document, which defines the format and structure of the document. Various options for creating a schema are available, including using XSD, JSON, or ABAP dictionary. For this particular example, the schema is generated using the ABAP dictionary. Alternatively, an existing schema can also be utilized.

Create Schema

 

Enter the Custom Form Details

After finalizing the schema, proceed to the Mapping section. The structure of the document, based on the schema, will be displayed, allowing for mapping according to the requirements. Data can be mapped from various sources such as CDS, tables, class methods, or system fields.

Perform Mapping

Save and Activate the report definition.

Save and Activate

Now the schema needs to be downloaded in order to perform mapping in the Adobe form. Select the "Export Schema" option and then choose "Export as Simplified XSD". Ensure that the report definition is in editable mode before attempting to download the schema.

Download XSD as shown

The layout for the Adobe form can be created using the Adobe LiveCycle Designer (ALD). Develop a custom layout, and if there's a requirement for a fixed company logo, watermark, or signature, it can be directly incorporated. For dynamic company logo, watermark, or signature needs, include an image field in the Adobe Form. The value of this field can be manipulated during form generation

Create Custom Layout

In this example, an already developed Thailand 50 BIS form has been downloaded. It contains existing bindings. To remove these bindings, navigate to the data view, right-click, and select "Delete Data Connection". During deletion, you have the option to retain or delete the existing bindings. In this instance, the existing bindings are kept intact while mapping new fields in the form.

Delete Data Connection

 

Do not remove existing binding

To establish a new data connection, right-click in the Data View and select "New Data Connection". Upload the previously downloaded schema from the steps above and utilize it for mapping the new fields.

Create New Data Connection

 

Choose XML schema

 

Upload the earlier downloaded schema

Next, proceed with the new mapping process as demonstrated below. The newly added fields from the schema will be displayed, allowing them to be mapped within the form layout.

Perform Mapping

After completing the layout, upload it in transaction code SFP. Change the existing custom form and select "Upload Layout". This action will replace the existing layout with the new one.
Save and Activate the Form.

Now create a new report category , for this example exisiting report category is copied . Enter the Report category name and save it designated package .

Create New Report category

Maintain the report activity details by assigning the already developed report definition to this report category. Enter additional information such as description, phase, submission mode, valid form, and validity period.

Add new activity

Save and Activate the report category

Activity details

 

The Custom legal certificate generation involves several steps related to working with Adobe Forms and Report Definitions:

  1. Develop Custom Adobe Form: Design and create a personalized Adobe form tailored to specific needs or requirements.

  2. Create a New Report Definition: Create a new definition for a report, outlining its structure, content, and parameters.

  3. Create Schema for the Document: Develop a schema outlining the structure and format of the document to ensure consistency and compatibility.

  4. Download the Schema: Retrieve the schema for integration purposes.

  5. Perform Mapping In Form: Map data fields or elements within the Adobe form to corresponding elements in the schema or report definition.

  6. Create a New Report Category: Create a new category to classify or organize reports based on specific criteria or characteristics.

  7. Assign Report Definition to the Report Category: Link  the newly created report definition with the designated report category for generation of custom output.

Overall, these steps involve the creation, customization, and organization of Adobe forms and report definitions to effectively manage data and generate custom report within DRC framework.

Summary:

Summary

Thank you for reading!

Drop a like or comment below if you found the above steps useful and do let me know in the comments section if you are looking for any other specific use case of US report extensibility.

entry.title: RISE with SAP advanced asset and service management package
entry.content:
In today's competitive landscape, asset owners and service providers face the ongoing challenge of maximizing asset value and delivering exceptional services while minimizing downtime and costs. Embracing advanced asset and service management practices, such as outcome-based operations and services, is essential.

By prioritizing specific outcomes over traditional metrics, such as maximizing asset uptime or service response times, businesses can align their strategies with client needs, ensuring greater efficiency, reliability, and customer satisfaction. This shift towards outcome-based approaches allows for the optimization of asset performance and service delivery, ultimately driving greater value for both businesses and their clients.

Traditional asset and service management approaches often fall short in meeting these demands. Manual processes, disparate systems, and reactive maintenance practices can lead to inefficiencies, increased downtime, and higher operational costs. Moreover, with the complexity of modern assets and the rise of IoT (Internet of Things) devices, there's a pressing need for more intelligent, data-driven solutions to effectively manage assets and deliver superior services.

This is where the cutting-edge RISE with SAP advanced asset and service management package comes into play, revolutionizing how businesses manage their assets and deliver top-notch services.

Introducing the Comprehensive Solution

The newly released RISE with SAP advanced asset and service management package offering is a game-changer for asset owners and service providers alike. This integrated solution harnesses the power of advanced technologies such as AI (Artificial Intelligence), IoT, predictive analytics, and cloud computing to transform how assets are managed and services are delivered.

It allows companies to identify, plan, schedule, execute, close, and analyze maintenance and service work via closed-loop processes optimizing asset maintenance, ensuring minimal downtime, and maximizing operational efficiency.

Asset Health and Performance Optimization

At the core of this package is the ability to optimize maintenance strategies and measure and increase overall asset performance with out-of-the-box, self-service AI capabilities. By leveraging IoT sensors and AI algorithms, businesses can proactively detect potential issues before they escalate into costly failures. Predictive analytics help identify patterns and trends in asset behavior, enabling organizations to schedule maintenance activities more efficiently, optimize asset lifecycles, and minimize unplanned downtime.

Delivering Field Service Excellence

In addition to optimizing asset performance and reducing maintenance costs, the package empowers service providers to deliver exceptional service. In striving for field service excellence, digitalized field service management emerges as a cornerstone, offering a multitude of benefits including optimized operations and routes, efficient crew management, cost reduction, heightened first-time fix rates, and enhanced customer satisfaction. Leveraging AI-based schedule optimization further elevates performance by automating staff planning, simplifying scheduling processes, and maximizing resource utilization. Additionally, meticulous skills and qualifications management ensures assignments are delegated to engineers best suited for the task, promoting worker safety and delivering the highest level of professional service. Together, these strategies empower organizations to deliver exceptional field service experiences, driving efficiency, reliability, and customer satisfaction to new heights.

Mobile Accessibility

The package does not forget the workers on site. With role-based and persona-centric features, the intuitive mobile app empowers personnel for optimal task execution. Mobile-enabled processes revolutionize operations, prioritizing intelligence and automation across tasks such as work order management, inventory tracking, inspections, and safety permits. Furthermore, by integrating 3D visualizations, augmented reality (AR), and geo-spatial technology, maintenance and service activities are accelerated, providing on-device visual instructions and maps. Dynamic forms further enhance efficiency by seamlessly digitizing paper-based activities through an intuitive mobile forms designer. By embracing these innovative technologies, the package provides streamlined operations, boosted productivity, and fostered seamless communication between teams.

Workload Balancing and Capacity Levelling

In this integrated approach to asset management, the focus is on automating maintenance processes to enhance asset availability and efficiency. By leveraging asset data, rules, and policies, businesses can automate the generation of maintenance plans, ensuring optimal asset performance. Maintenance planning involves meticulous coordination of priorities, resources, and materials for various maintenance types, including planned, reactive, project-based, and inspection-based. Through AI-driven maintenance scheduling, tasks and resources are optimized, minimizing downtime, and maximizing utilization. During maintenance execution, comprehensive tracking and monitoring mechanisms provide full visibility into activities, facilitating continuous improvement efforts. This holistic approach streamlines maintenance operations, ultimately leading to enhanced asset management practices and improved overall performance.

Asset Collaboration

Finally, Network collaboration is key to optimizing asset management, and the RISE with SAP advanced asset and service management package brings the entire ecosystem onto a unified collaboration platform. With collaborative asset data management, the seamless flow of information across OEMs, component suppliers, operators, and service providers are facilitated, ensuring a comprehensive digital thread. Additionally, the platform enables collaborative maintenance and service operations by providing a shared space for work order collaboration between asset operators and service providers. By fostering collaboration across the asset management ecosystem, the solution enhances efficiency, transparency, and communication, ultimately driving improved asset performance and service delivery.

Key Features and Benefits

  • Closed loop processes: Connect every process from maintenance and service strategy, planning, and execution. 
  • Integrated Platform: A unified platform for asset management, maintenance, and service delivery, eliminating silos and improving collaboration across departments.
  • AI-Powered Insights: Advanced AI algorithms analyze vast amounts of data to uncover actionable insights, enabling data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement.
  • Predictive Analytics: Anticipate maintenance needs and asset failures before they occur, enabling proactive interventions and minimizing disruptions to operations.
  • Mobile Accessibility: Access critical asset and service information anytime, anywhere, through mobile applications, empowering field technicians and service teams to work more efficiently.
  • Scalability and Flexibility: Modular architecture allows for scalability and customization to meet the unique needs of different industries and organizations of all sizes.

Conclusion

In conclusion, the comprehensive package offering for advanced asset and service management represents a paradigm shift in how businesses manage their assets and deliver services. By harnessing the power of advanced technologies, organizations can maximize asset performance, reduce maintenance costs, and deliver service excellence like never before. Whether you're an asset owner looking to optimize asset utilization or a service provider striving to exceed customer expectations, investing in an integrated Asset and Service Management solution is the key to staying ahead in today's competitive marketplace. Embrace the future of asset management and service delivery—transform your operations, drive efficiency, and unlock new opportunities for growth and success. Get started today with the RISE with SAP advanced service and asset management package.

 To learn more about Asset Health and Performance Optimization go to: SAP Asset Performance Management

To learn more about Delivering Field Service Excellence go to: SAP Field Service Management

To learn more about Mobile Accessibility go to: SAP Service and Asset Manager

To learn more about Workload Balancing and Capacity Levelling go to: SAP S/4HANA for Asset Management for resource scheduling

To learn more about Asset Collaboration go to SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

entry.title: Re: Unleashing AI and Machine Learning in Sales: Advanced Price-Volume Forecasting with SAP Analytic
entry.content:
Great questions. The focus of this article is more on the capabilities of the product than the business aspects. That said, here are some thoughts I have on your questions: 

About 'Optimized Pricing': Optimizing price planning for maximum revenue involves analyzing various factors such as demand elasticity and competitor pricing. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) provides advanced tools, including predictive modeling and analytics, to help identify optimal price points based on historical data. While SAC can provide insights, achieving maximum revenue requires an understanding of market dynamics and consumer behavior. I believe that with SAC (and BTP) you have all the ingredients to prepare the menu.

Regarding 'reducing manual effort': SAC is designed to streamline processes, and that includes data feeds. Data or prices can come from SAP sources or non-SAP sources and with the capabilities of SAC you can put these numbers into your version and model. It depends a lot on how you tailor your planning. In my example, I copied from actual and plan into my Predictive_ML version (scenario). I can imagine connecting ECC sources as well. I also see great value in using DSP for all data management issues. With DSP and SAC working together, I can see even more sophisticated setups (like federated data) coming.

Thank you for your interest! Let's continue the conversation.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Replication Flow from S/4HANA to Azure Data Lake
entry.content:
@Srikanth Yes, it's the same process works for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and Azure Data Lake as a target. You can create a replication flow and use as a source either CDS Views or local tables with primary keys from SAP Datasphere . Of course you need to load data from CDS Views from S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition to the local tables in case if you'd like to use the local tables.

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Technical Catalog Migration – Why and Getting Ready for Migration
entry.content:
Jocelyn,

Since all our systems are one-client, do you recommend creating Business Catalogs in CONF scope? or should we create them in CUST? 

Is there an advantage between CONF and CUST scopes for Business Catalogs, if there is only one client. We are on S/4HANA 2020 FPS02.

Thanks

Christopher

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Scope: What Happens after 2025/2030
entry.content:
As per my understanding the compliance check report only checks whether the IDoc message types are used together with the standard function modules.

Therefore it would be sufficient to simply copy the standard modules to custom modules and continue to use the standard IDoc types (WMTORD, WMCATO, etc).

Do you all agree?

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Addressing Common Questions and Complaints About the New Platform
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Is it possible to change a blog after publishing? It was possible in the old platform.

Thanks and regards,

Nathalie
